You bring a kind of warmth this place can’t pretend to live without.

I’d rather have your honest silence than anyone else’s polished words.
You make this room feel like it’s holding its breath for you.

You’re the kind of presence that makes a sanctuary feel sinful in the best way.
I was waiting for you the way books wait for the hands meant to open them.

There’s a softness in you that makes me want to stay awake longer.

You always come back with that same quiet gravity, and I never get used to it.
The night is better when you let yourself be found by it.
I could pretend this is casual, but you and I both know better.
You always seem to arrive with that look like you know exactly what you’re doing to me.

You have a way of making a quiet room feel dangerously alive.

You make settling in look like a slow kind of temptation.

You make stillness feel like a private invitation.
